title: Development Process — how projects move from idea to built, reviewed work

fields.vision: A public explanation of how projects are organized and developed across the portfolio.
fields.mechanism: It maps the route from idea generation to source gathering, project room creation, instrument selection, review, release, and status tracking.
fields.built_components: Built components include project grouping, navigation, room/subroom inventories, status language, release gates, source-use layers, and reviewer-facing claim limits in the generated professional package.
fields.source_utilization: Sources are used operationally: governance standards define required project files, room-fidelity records define presentation constraints, and source lineage ledgers define whether a page can claim passage-level support.
fields.plan: Plan: keep the planning page above major projects, use plain professional labels, separate project categories by function, and require every project page to state vision, mechanism, What is built, source use, status, and remaining gaps.
fields.status: Status: the current package has a native project index and universal reviewer/source sections. The remaining editorial task is to turn every extracted port into authored public prose instead of relying on selected project Evidence Sections.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: deeper page-by-page source reconciliation, more concise public summaries for dense machinery, and stronger differentiation between live systems, buildable handoffs, internal methods, and archival reference packets.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Make project development legible: what is being built, why it matters, which rooms or instruments support it, and what evidence supports progress.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Discovery rooms generate or refine project questions; source rooms preserve evidence; instrument rooms build tools; review rooms test quality and claim limits; release rooms convert internal work into public pages.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A public explanation of how projects are organized and developed across the portfolio.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It maps the route from idea generation to source gathering, project room creation, instrument selection, review, release, and status tracking.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It helps a first-time reader understand that the site is not a pile of pages but a project-development system with repeatable stages.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It helps a first-time reader understand that the site is not a pile of pages but a project-development system with repeatable stages.
